steps.title: Substitute x = 58 into the model
steps.content: The equation $y=0.67x+2.6$ predicts the number of larvae $y$ from the number of worker ants $x$. Since the colony has $58$ worker ants, plug in $x=58$: $$y=0.67(58)+2.6.$$

steps.title: Check each choice
steps.content:
• A) $41$ — matches $41.46$ rounded. Correct.
  • B) $61$ — would need $y\approx 61$, but we got $41.46$. Eliminated.
  • C) $83$ — this is roughly $58+\text{something}$, but the model is not just $x+c$. Eliminated.
  • D) $190$ — far too large; $0.67(58)$ is well under $40$. Eliminated.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
